Building an Autoupdate Package

To build an autoupdate package for submission, select Build from the Action menu. Before you build, it is a good idea to check the following.

  • The file default.xbe exists. Every autoupdate package requires the file default.xbe. It does not matter whether this file has changed.
  • Every XBE file is linked with XOnlines.lib. All XBE files in the autoupdate must be linked to this library, not just default.xbe.
  • No XBE is linked to a debug XTL. Debug versions of the XTL cannot be used in the final build of an autoupdate package.
  • No XBE is linked to a debug version of XOnlines.lib.
  • Major XTL version number match. The XTLs used by both the original game and the autoupdate must have the same major version numbers. The minor version numbers do not have to be the same.
  • The XTLs include all mandatory QFEs. The XTLs you use for your autoupdate must have all of the mandatory QFEs for that major version number.

Note    Certificate information is not required for the updated executable files in your autoupdate package. It is added after you submit the autoupdate.

If you build your autoupdate more than a few months after the original game release, you will probably get a warning indicating that some of the XTLs you're using are not approved. This occurs because the XTLs used by both the original game and the autoupdate must have the same major version numbers, and the major version numbers expire after a few months. Getting this warning does not prevent you from building your autoupdate. However, it is strongly recommended that you use the ImageCert tool to verify that this is the reason that you are getting the warning. If ImageCert indicates that the XTLs your autoupdate uses are expired, it can still pass certification. If ImageCert gives any other reason that your XTLs are not approved, the autoupdate won't pass certification. It's best to address this problem before submission.

It is possible to use the XLAST autoupdate feature to add data files that were not originally distributed on the DVD. To accomplish this:

  1. Create a zero-byte file with the desired name in the original game directory.
  2. Put the new file in the updated game directory.
  3. Manually add the updated file to the XLAST autoupdate project.

Note    An autoupdate cannot add new XBEs. It can only add new data files.

In addition, you can use the XLAST autoupdate feature to update non-XBE files that were originally distributed on the DVD. To accomplish this:

  1. Manually add the updated file to the XLAST autoupdate project.
  2. Change all code that opens the file to reference T:\U$\file instead of D:\file.
  3. Use the content signature API functions to ensure T:\U$\file is not corrupt.

Note    It is not recommended that you update large data files because a copy of the entire updated data file is placed on the Xbox hard disk.

There is a known issue in the XLAST autoupdate feature that improperly allows you to add files that are outside the original game build directory. In this situation, XLAST generates the following errors when building the project.

Building autoupdate
Error generating file-list.
Build Failed

This same issue causes XLAST to generate the following error messages when testing the project locally.

Installing files on Xbox
Install Failed. Please ensure you have configured a default Xbox and it is turned on.
Install Succeeded
Unpublished work. © 2000 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
